summary: Obadiah pronounces God's judgment on Edom for violence against Judah and gloating over Jerusalem's fall, while Psalms 82 and 83 call God to judge corrupt rulers and a coalition of enemy nations, both closing with the declaration that the LORD alone is sovereign over the earth.
psalm_rows_html:
PsalmTypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
82Prophetic / ImprecatoryAsaphA divine council scene in which God confronts unjust earthly rulers and judges (called 'gods') for failing to defend the weakGod as the supreme and only just Judge; all earthly authority is accountable to himv.6 — "I said, 'You are gods, sons of the Most High, all of you; nevertheless, like men you shall die"Jesus quotes v.6 in John 10:34 to answer the charge of blasphemy, arguing that if Scripture calls human judges 'gods,' how much more can he claim divine sonship
83Lament / ImprecatoryAsaphA coalition of ten surrounding nations conspires to destroy Israel and wipe out the name of God's peoplePrayer for God to vindicate his name by defeating those who seek to erase Israel; the LORD's honor is at stake in his people's survivalv.18 — "that they may know that you alone, whose name is the LORD, are the Most High over all the earth"The theme of all nations acknowledging the LORD's supremacy points forward to the eschatological vision of Revelation and Paul's doxology in Romans 11:36
